headlines.title: Mitch McConnell says he suffered fall, was unconscious in first statement since hospitalization
headlines.description: McConnell, R-Ky., said he would return to the Senate but didn’t give a date.

headlines.title: Sen. Lindsey Graham dies at 71 after ‘brief and sudden illness’
headlines.description: President Donald Trump led tributes to Graham, a leading Republican voice on defense and foreign policy, describing him as a “true American Patriot.”

headlines.title: Questions about who will replace Sen. Graham as new image of Sen. McConnell released
headlines.description: Tonight lawmakers from both parties are remembering the life and legacy of Sen. Lindsey Graham (R-S.C.), who died at the age of 71. There are new questions tonight about who will replace him in South Carolina, and growing scrutiny about fellow Republican Mitch McConnell’s health after a newly released statement and photo of the 84-year-old GOP senator. NBC News’ Ryan Nobles reports.

headlines.title: Graham makes final remarks in Ukraine a day before this death
headlines.description: Sen. Lindsey Graham met with Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy and made public remarks during a trip to Kyiv just one day before his death. Graham said he was focused on advancing legislation aimed at imposing sanctions on Russian oil.

headlines.title: Netanyahu reacts to Ro Khanna saying he was detained in the West Bank: ‘We are a nation of laws’
headlines.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu joins Meet the Press after Rep Ro Khanna (D-Calif.) says he was held by Israeli settlers during his trip to the West Bank.

headlines.title: State Assemblyman Alex Bores speaks on AI and politics
headlines.description: State Assemblyman Alex Bores, a candidate for New York's 12th Congressional District, speaks with NBC News’ Gadi Schwartz about why some top artificial intelligence companies are opposing his candidacy and whether AI should play a role in politics.

headlines.title: Republicans scramble to find a replacement for Sen. Lindsey Graham ahead of the midterm elections
headlines.description: President Donald Trump told NBC News he has someone in mind he’d like to replace the late South Carolina senator but declined to name the person.

headlines.title: Graham Platner officially withdraws from the Maine Senate race
headlines.description: Embattled candidate Graham Platner officially dropped out of Maine’s U.S.

headlines.title: Judge tosses remaining Jan. 6 Proud Boys convictions after DOJ request
headlines.description: The convictions against the four Proud Boys are some of the last remaining cases to stand after President Donald Trump’s sweeping pardons from January 2025.

headlines.title: On the same day, a Florida airport and a Tennessee bridge are renamed after Trump
headlines.description: Palm Beach International Airport is now President Donald J. Trump International Airport, and a Jefferson County bridge is the President Donald J. Trump Bridge.

headlines.title: Trump ousts remaining members of the Election Assistance Commission
headlines.description: The dismissals hamstring a key bipartisan agency just months before the midterms.

headlines.title: Ministry founder Victor Marx wins Republican primary for Colorado governor
headlines.description: Marx, who has gained notice due to a claim he killed a man when he was a child, will advance to the general election, NBC News projects.
